originalTitle: Analysis-SpaceX debut draws a crowd, but few recent hot IPOs outpace the market
summary: Wall Street is abuzz with next month's expected blockbuster debut of Elon Musk's rocket and satellite maker SpaceX, but few of the biggest IPOs in recent years have paid off for investors who bought in when the deals came to market. A Reuters analysis ‌of the 50 IPOs with the highest valuations in the past five years shows that investors would have been better off buying an S&P 500 ‌index fund about three-quarters of the time. An investor who bought each of the IPOs tracked ​by Reuters would be up an average of 27% through May 21.
content:
May 26 (Reuters) - Wall Street is abuzz with next month's expected blockbuster debut of Elon Musk's rocket and satellite maker SpaceX, but few of the biggest IPOs in recent years have paid off for investors who bought in when the deals came to market.

A Reuters analysis ‌of the 50 IPOs with the highest valuations in the past five years shows that investors would have been better off buying an S&P 500 ‌index fund about three-quarters of the time. The data underscores the difficulty of finding bargains among companies whose valuations have often surged long before the stock's debut.

How have major IPOs performed versus S&P 500 recently?

What makes SpaceX's upcoming IPO valuation concerning?

Which recent IPOs have been biggest winners and losers?

Why do high-valuation IPOs typically underperform the market?

An investor who bought each of the IPOs tracked ​by Reuters would be up an average of 27% through May 21. That compares to an average gain of 53% in the S&P 500 over those same periods. The analysis assumes the buyer would be able to purchase shares at the IPO price - often not possible for a retail investor - or simply buy the broad-market S&P.

Historical returns for investors buying during the frenzied first day of trading of a stock fare even worse, the analysis showed.

"It's difficult to make money unless you're in the early stages of these things and buying these ‌things before the IPO," said Dennis Dick, a proprietary trader ⁠at Triple D Trading.

SpaceX's debut is expected to be followed by OpenAI and Anthropic, tapping into demand for AI-related companies that has sent the U.S. stock market to record highs.

Set to trade under the ticker 'SPCX', SpaceX filed its prospectus on Wednesday, ⁠with a share sale potentially as early as June 11. Founder Elon Musk is making some shares available to retail investors through Robinhood, SoFi and other trading platforms that would allow them to get in at a lower price.

The space exploration company is expected to target a $1.75 trillion valuation that would dwarf all previous Wall Street stock listings, but the Reuters analysis shows that ​such ​superlatives are no guarantee investors will make money.

University of Florida professor Jay Ritter, who studies IPOs, ​said that while most public listings underperform the S&P 500 over ‌the long run, companies with particularly high valuations as measured by price-to-sales tend to fare the worst.

At a $1.75 trillion valuation, SpaceX's price-to-sales ratio would be nearly 100, compared to AI heavyweight Nvidia 's price-to-sales ratio of 24. SpaceX lost nearly $5 billion last year.

"Every one of these companies where investors are willing to pay a very high price-to-sales ratio has a compelling story for why the future potentially can be really bright," Ritter said. "But, you know, stuff could go wrong."

Among the IPOs analyzed, AI-related chip designers Astera Labs and Arm Holdings have been the biggest winners. Astera has surged over 700% since its 2024 IPO, while Arm has soared about 400% since ‌its 2023 debut. Both of those performances outpace the S&P.

Cerebras Systems, another AI chip designer, soared 52% ​from its May 14 IPO price; it is down around 27% from its first intraday high.

Electric car maker Rivian Automotive ​has slumped 82% since its IPO in 2021 ​that briefly made it the second-most valuable U.S. automaker. The company continues to lose money for every car it builds, and is burning around $1 billion in cash every quarter.

Shares in design software firm Figma nearly quadrupled in their first trading session last July. But with investors worried that generative AI could commoditize ​Figma's technology, its stock is down 35% from the $33 IPO ‌price.

Even the hottest offerings can lag. Chinese e-commerce company Alibaba, which Reuters did not include in its analysis, holds the record for the largest U.S. ​IPO by valuation. Touted as the "Amazon of China," its shares have doubled since its 2014 Wall Street debut, during which time the S&P 500 ​has returned over 300%.

(Reporting by Noel Randewich, editing by Colin Barr and David Gaffen)
chineseTitle: 分析——SpaceX首次亮相吸引众人目光，但近期热门IPO鲜有跑赢市场的
chineseSummary: 华尔街对埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 旗下火箭和卫星制造商 SpaceX 预计将于下个月的重磅首次亮相热闹非凡，但近年来规模最大的 IPO 几乎没有为上市时买入的投资者带来回报。路透社对过去五年中估值最高的 50 家 IPO 的分析显示，大约四分之三的时间里，投资者购买标普 500 指数基金会更好。截至 5 月 21 日，购买路透社追踪的每笔 IPO 的投资者的平均股价将上涨 27%。
chineseContent:
5 月 26 日（路透社）——华尔街对埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 旗下火箭和卫星制造商 SpaceX 预计将于下个月重磅上市的消息议论纷纷，但近年来规模最大的 IPO 几乎没有为上市时买入的投资者带来回报。

路透社对过去五年中估值最高的 50 家 IPO 的分析显示，大约四分之三的时间里，投资者购买标普 500 指数基金会更好。这些数据突显出，在那些估值往往早在股票首次亮相之前就飙升的公司中寻找便宜货是很困难的。

最近主要 IPO 与标准普尔 500 指数相比表现如何？

SpaceX 即将到来的 IPO 估值为何令人担忧？

最近哪些 IPO 是最大的赢家和输家？

为什么高估值IPO通常表现逊于市场？

截至 5 月 21 日，购买路透社追踪的每一次 IPO 的投资者的平均股价将上涨 27%。相比之下，同期标准普尔 500 指数的平均涨幅为 53%。该分析假设买家能够以 IPO 价格购买股票（这对于散户投资者来说通常是不可能的），或者干脆购买大市场标准普尔指数。

分析显示，投资者在股票首日疯狂交易期间买入的历史回报甚至更糟。

Triple D Trading 的自营交易员丹尼斯·迪克 (Dennis Dick) 表示：“除非你处于这些事情的早期阶段并在 IPO 之前购买这些东西，否则很难赚钱。”

OpenAI 和 Anthropic 预计将紧随 SpaceX 首次亮相，利用人工智能相关公司的需求，这些公司已将美国股市推向历史新高。

SpaceX 周三提交了招股说明书，股票交易代码为“SPCX”，最早可能于 6 月 11 日出售股票。创始人埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 正在通过 Robinhood、SoFi 和其他交易平台向散户投资者出售部分股票，这将使他们能够以较低的价格买入。

这家太空探索公司的估值目标预计为 1.75 万亿美元，这将使所有之前上市的华尔街股票相形见绌，但路透社的分析表明，这样的最高估值并不能保证投资者会赚钱。

研究 IPO 的佛罗里达大学教授杰伊·里特 (Jay Ritter) 表示，虽然从长远来看，大多数公开上市的公司表现逊于标准普尔 500 指数，但以市销率衡量估值特别高的公司往往表现最差。

以 1.75 万亿美元的估值计算，SpaceX 的市销率将接近 100，而 AI 巨头英伟达的市销率为 24。SpaceX 去年亏损了近 50 亿美元。

里特说：“投资者愿意支付非常高的市销率的这些公司中的每一家都有一个令人信服的故事来说明为什么未来可能非常光明。” “但是，你知道，事情可能会出错。”

在分析的IPO中，人工智能相关芯片设计公司Astera Labs和Arm Holdings是最大赢家。自 2024 年 IPO 以来，Astera 已飙升超过 700%，而 Arm 自 2023 年首次亮相以来已飙升约 400%。这两项表现都超过了标准普尔指数。

另一家人工智能芯片设计商 Cerebras Systems 较 5 月 14 日 IPO 价格飙升 52%；它比第一个盘中高点下跌了约 27%。

电动汽车制造商 Rivian Automotive 自 2021 年 IPO 以来已下跌 82%，一度成为美国第二大市值汽车制造商。该公司每生产一辆汽车就持续亏损，并且每个季度烧掉大约 10 亿美元的现金。

设计软件公司 Figma 的股价在去年 7 月的第一个交易日中几乎翻了两番。但由于投资者担心生成式人工智能可能会使 Figma 的技术商品化，其股价较 33 美元的 IPO 价格下跌了 35%。

即使是最热门的产品也可能会滞后。中国电子商务公司阿里巴巴（路透社未将其纳入其分析中）保持着按估值计算的美国最大 IPO 记录。被誉为“中国亚马逊”的其股价自 2014 年在华尔街首次亮相以来已经翻了一番，在此期间标准普尔 500 指数的回报率已超过 300%。

（诺埃尔·兰德维奇报道，科林·巴尔和大卫·加芬编辑）
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
